title: 📢 Midterm Exam Schedule Released
content:
Midterm Examination Schedule

Date: March 20th, 2026

Time: 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM

Location: Main Hall (Room 101-105)


Important Instructions:

  • Bring your student ID card
  • Arrive 15 minutes early
  • No electronic devices allowed
  • Calculators are permitted


For any special accommodations, please contact the Exams Office by March 15th.

title: Week 5 Lecture Notes Now Available
content:
Database Systems - Week 5

The lecture notes for Week 5 have been uploaded. This week covers:

  • Normalization: 1NF, 2NF, 3NF, BCNF
  • SQL Optimization: Indexing and query planning
  • Transaction Management: ACID properties


Note: The tutorial for this week will be held on Friday instead of Thursday due to the holiday.

title: 🌟 Guest Lecture: AI in Modern Education
content:
Special Guest Lecture

We are excited to announce a guest lecture by Dr. Sarah Johnson from Stanford University.


Topic: The Future of AI in Education

Date: March 18th, 2026

Time: 3:00 PM - 5:00 PM

Location: Auditorium B


"AI will transform how we teach and learn. Join us to explore the possibilities!"


This lecture is mandatory for all CS students. Refreshments will be provided.

title: ⚠️ System Maintenance This Weekend
content:
Scheduled Maintenance Notice

The LMS will be unavailable on Saturday, March 16th from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM for system upgrades.


What's being updated:

  • New quiz engine with better math support
  • Improved discussion forums
  • Security patches


We apologize for any inconvenience. The system will be back online by 6:00 AM.

title: Project Submission Guidelines
content:
Final Year Project - Important Update

The project submission deadline has been extended to April 5th, 2026.


Submission requirements:

  1. Project report (PDF format)
  2. Source code (ZIP archive)
  3. Presentation slides (PPT or PDF)
  4. Demo video (optional, max 5 minutes)


Use the submission portal on the LMS by 11:59 PM on the deadline date.


For any questions, attend the Q&A session this Friday at 2 PM in Room 302.
